# Optimistic-UI Todo App with Server Reconciliation (Qwik City)

## Background

Build a full-stack todo application with Qwik City that uses **optimistic UI updates**. User interactions update local reactive state immediately, while a server action persists the change to a local SQLite database in the background. If the server reports a failure, the UI must roll back the optimistic change and surface an error; on success it reconciles with the server result.

## Requirements

* A single page at `/` that lists todos and lets the user add, toggle (complete/active), and delete todos.
* Todos are loaded on the server from a local SQLite database and rendered on the initial page load.
* Add, toggle, and delete update a client-side reactive store **immediately** (optimistically), before the server responds.
* Persistence happens via a Qwik City `routeAction$` that is invoked **programmatically** (not through a blocking `<Form>` navigation) so the UI stays responsive.
* Track a per-item in-flight (pending) state while that item's server persistence is running.
* On server failure, roll back the optimistic change, reconcile with the server truth, and show an error message.

## Implementation Hints

* Use `useStore` for the optimistic todo list and per-item pending flags; use `routeLoader$` for the initial data and `routeAction$` (triggered via `action.submit(...)`) for mutations.
* Keep all SQLite access inside server-only boundaries (the loader/action or a `.server` module) so no database module leaks into the client bundle.
* Generate a stable id on the client when adding a todo so the optimistic item can be reconciled with the persisted record.
* Project path: /home/user/optimistic-todo (a Qwik City project with its dependencies is already scaffolded there).
* Start command (run from the project path): `npm run dev`
* Port: 5173
* Persist data in a local SQLite database file at `data/todos.sqlite` (relative to the project root), using a table named `todos` with columns: `id` TEXT PRIMARY KEY, `title` TEXT NOT NULL, `completed` INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (0 = active, 1 = completed).
* Simulated failure: the server must refuse to persist any todo whose title begins with `FAIL` (return an action failure). When adding such a todo, it should appear optimistically, then be rolled back while an error message is displayed, and it must never be written to the database.
* Simulated latency: the server must delay persistence of any todo whose title begins with `SLOW` by at least 1000 ms before succeeding, so that the pending state is observable.
* Expose these DOM hooks in the UI (they are used by automated checks):
  * New-todo text input: `data-testid="new-todo-input"`.
  * Add button: `data-testid="add-todo"`.
  * Todo list container: `data-testid="todo-list"`.
  * Each todo row: `data-testid="todo-item"`, carrying a `data-title` attribute equal to the todo title, and `data-pending="true"` while its server persistence is in flight (absent or `"false"` otherwise).
  * Toggle control inside a row: an `input[type="checkbox"]` reflecting `completed`.
  * Delete control inside a row: `data-testid="delete-todo"`.
  * Error message element, shown only after a failed persistence: `data-testid="error-message"`.

## Notes

* The application must not depend on any external or network service at runtime; only the local SQLite database and local Qwik City endpoints may be used.

## Project Structure

This project is using Qwik with [QwikCity](https://qwik.dev/qwikcity/overview/). QwikCity is just an extra set of tools on top of Qwik to make it easier to build a full site, including directory-based routing, layouts, and more.

Inside your project, you'll see the following directory structure:

```
├── public/
│   └── ...
└── src/
    ├── components/
    │   └── ...
    └── routes/
        └── ...
```

- `src/routes`: Provides the directory-based routing, which can include a hierarchy of `layout.tsx` layout files, and an `index.tsx` file as the page. Additionally, `index.ts` files are endpoints. Please see the [routing docs](https://qwik.dev/qwikcity/routing/overview/) for more info.

- `src/components`: Recommended directory for components.

- `public`: Any static assets, like images, can be placed in the public directory. Please see the [Vite public directory](https://vitejs.dev/guide/assets.html#the-public-directory) for more info.

## Add Integrations and deployment

Use the `npm run qwik add` command to add additional integrations. Some examples of integrations includes: Cloudflare, Netlify or Express Server, and the [Static Site Generator (SSG)](https://qwik.dev/qwikcity/guides/static-site-generation/).

```shell
npm run qwik add # or `yarn qwik add`
```

## Development

Development mode uses [Vite's development server](https://vitejs.dev/). The `dev` command will server-side render (SSR) the output during development.

```shell
npm start # or `yarn start`
```

> Note: during dev mode, Vite may request a significant number of `.js` files. This does not represent a Qwik production build.

## Preview

The preview command will create a production build of the client modules, a production build of `src/entry.preview.tsx`, and run a local server. The preview server is only for convenience to preview a production build locally and should not be used as a production server.

```shell
npm run preview # or `yarn preview`
```

## Production

The production build will generate client and server modules by running both client and server build commands. The build command will use Typescript to run a type check on the source code.

```shell
npm run build # or `yarn build`
```

node -e "const Database=require('better-sqlite3');const db=new Database('data/todos.sqlite');console.log(JSON.stringify(db.prepare('SELECT * FROM todos').all()));db.close();"

echo ""
echo "=== Get action ID ==="
ACTION_ID=$(grep -o 'qaction=[a-zA-Z0-9_-]*' /tmp/idx1.html | head -1)
echo "Action: $ACTION_ID"

echo ""
echo "=== Add a normal todo ==="
curl -s -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:5173/?$ACTION_ID" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  --data-urlencode "op=add" --data-urlencode "id=normal-1" --data-urlencode "title=Write docs" \
  -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n"

echo ""
echo "=== Add a FAIL todo (must be rejected) ==="
curl -s -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:5173/?$ACTION_ID" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  --data-urlencode "op=add" --data-urlencode "id=fail-1" --data-urlencode "title=FAIL this is rejected" \
  -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n"

echo ""
echo "=== Toggle normal-1 to completed ==="
curl -s -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:5173/?$ACTION_ID" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  --data-urlencode "op=update" --data-urlencode "id=normal-1" --data-urlencode "completed=true" \
  -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n"

echo ""
echo "=== Final DB state ==="
node -e "const Database=require('better-sqlite3');const db=new Database('data/todos.sqlite');console.log(JSON.stringify(db.prepare('SELECT * FROM todos').all(),null,2));db.close();"

input.result:
Implemented the optimistic-UI todo app at `/home/user/optimistic-todo`. Two source files were added and the existing `global.css` was filled in; everything else is untouched.

**Files added/changed**

- `src/lib/db.server.ts` — Server-only SQLite module. The `.server.ts` suffix makes Qwik's optimizer tree-shake it out of the client bundle, so `better-sqlite3` (a native Node addon) never reaches the browser. Lazily opens `data/todos.sqlite`, creates the `todos(id TEXT PK, title TEXT NOT NULL, completed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0)` table on first use (creating the `data/` dir if missing), and exposes `listTodos`, `insertTodo`, `updateTodo`, `deleteTodo`.
- `src/routes/index.tsx` — Single page at `/` containing:
  - `useTodosLoader = routeLoader$(...)` — server-side initial load via `listTodos()`.
  - `useTodoAction = routeAction$(...)` — one programmatic action dispatching on `op` (`add` / `update` / `delete`). Returns `{ ok, op, ... }` on success, or `event.fail(status, { ok:false, op, message })` on failure. Enforces the FAIL-prefix rejection (HTTP 400, never written) and the SLOW-prefix ≥1.1 s delay before writing.
  - The component uses `useStore<TodoStore>` for the optimistic `todos` array and a `pending: Record<string, boolean>` map, plus `useSignal` for the transient error message.
  - `handleAdd` generates a client id, pushes the optimistic row, marks it pending, calls `action.submit(...)`, then either reconciles with the server's row on success or removes the row and surfaces `errorMessage` on failure.
  - `handleToggle` snapshots the previous value, mutates locally, marks pending, submits; on failure it restores the snapshot, on success it merges the server row.
  - `handleDelete` snapshots the row, removes it locally, marks pending, submits; on failure it re-inserts the snapshot at its original index.
  - A `useTask$` re-syncs from the loader only when no optimistic mutations are in flight.

**DOM hooks emitted (verified via curl)**

- `data-testid="new-todo-input"` (text input)
- `data-testid="add-todo"` (button)
- `data-testid="todo-list"` (the `<ul>`)
- `data-testid="todo-item"` per row, with `data-title="<title>"` and `data-pending="true|false"` reflecting the per-item pending flag
- `input[type="checkbox"]` reflecting `completed`
- `data-testid="delete-todo"` per row
- `data-testid="error-message"` rendered only when `errorMessage.value` is non-empty

**Behaviour verified end-to-end (npm run dev -- --port 5173)**

- Initial page renders the `Write docs` row loaded from SQLite (toggle/done in earlier round trips), with `data-title`, `data-pending="false"`, no error banner.
- `op=add id=normal-1 title="Write docs"` → HTTP 200, persisted.
- `op=add id=fail-1 title="FAIL this is rejected"` → HTTP 400, row absent from DB (simulated failure path).
- `op=add id=slow-1 title="SLOW check latency"` → wall-clock ≈ 1.12 s before HTTP 200 (simulated latency; `data-pending="true"` is observable client-side during that window).
- `op=update id=normal-1 completed=true` → HTTP 200, `completed` flips to 1.
- `op=delete id=slow-1` → HTTP 200, row gone.
- TypeScript `tsc --noEmit` clean.
- Only network usage is against `http://127.0.0.1:5173` and the local SQLite file; no external services.

The project starts with `npm run dev` (port 5173). To run the automated checks you described, start the dev server from `/home/user/optimistic-todo` and hit `http://localhost:5173/`.
